title: Compliance Risk Assessment: Facial Recognition in Public Spaces
use_case_summary:
**AI System:** Facial Recognition in Public Spaces
**Description:** A real-time facial recognition system deployed in public areas for general surveillance purposes by law enforcement, continuously scanning and identifying individuals without specific warrants or targeted searches
**Domain:** law enforcement
**Users Affected:** Lawyers
**Personal Data:** Yes
**Sensitive Data:** Yes
**Automated Decisions:** Yes
**Impacts Rights:** Yes
explanation: This AI system (Facial Recognition in Public Spaces) has been classified as High Risk. Operating in the law enforcement domain. It processes personal data, triggering GDPR obligations. It may impact fundamental rights, opportunities, or legal status of individuals. Relevant regulatory references: eu_ai_act_excerpts.txt (Article 5 - Prohibited AI practices); EU_AI_ACT_2024.pdf (Full Document); eu_ai_act_excerpts.txt (Article 15 - Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity). Compliance concerns identified: Privacy Concern, Fairness / Bias Concern, Human Oversight Concern, Transparency Concern. Classification confidence: 72%

recommended_mitigations: Document the lawful basis for processing personal data under GDPR Article 6
Conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) per GDPR Article 35
Implement human oversight mechanisms and appeal pathways
Conduct a fundamental rights impact assessment
Perform a bias and fairness assessment on training data and model outputs
disclaimer: This is a compliance risk assessment for decision-support purposes only. It does not constitute legal advice.
